formula_note: Taliban deaths anchored at 400 x 0.25 = 100; low/high widen this to 80-120 to reflect kill-claim uncertainty, then add 12 NATO deaths.
uncertainty_note: Likely undercounts Afghan-security fatalities because no separate ANSF death total was found for the narrow episode window.
quality_note: Derived from explicit insurgent-kill and NATO-fatality claims, but ANSF losses are incomplete.

formula_note: Low/high preserve the published range; best is the midpoint 22.
uncertainty_note: Source disagreement is explicit; no attempt was made to mechanically average across hidden underlying datasets.
quality_note: Strong for a short episode, with an explicit source-bounded range.

formula_note: No separate unknown-status death series found for the episode.
uncertainty_note: Unknown-status deaths were strictly excluded rather than silently allocated.
quality_note: Null by design under strict exclusion.

formula_note: Average directly exposed armed personnel = (8,000/9,000/10,000 core ISAF-ANSF + 400 Taliban) x 18/30 month.
uncertainty_note: Broader 15,000-force central-Helmand figure was treated as context, not the denominator, because it covered a larger support belt than the chosen theater footprint.
quality_note: Good denominator triangulation for a short, coherent clear phase.

formula_note: Average civilian presence scenarios of 40,000 / 60,000 / 80,000 over 18 days, converted using 30-day months.
uncertainty_note: Displacement reporting combines Marjah and Nad Ali and does not create a clean daily time series, so the best estimate is scenario-based.
quality_note: Reasonable person-month scenarios anchored to contemporaneous population and displacement reporting.

formula_note: Low=(mil_low/mil_pm_high)/(civ_high/civ_pm_low); best=(mil_best/mil_pm_best)/(civ_best/civ_pm_best); high=(mil_high/mil_pm_low)/(civ_low/civ_pm_high).
uncertainty_note: Even the low scenario remains >1, but the spread is wide because both insurgent kill claims and civilian exposure are uncertain.
quality_note: Interpret as a descriptive rate ratio for the clear phase, not a legal finding.

formula_note: Best anchored on the official claim of 150 Taliban killed in the recapture phase; low discounts official kill claims heavily; high allows additional organized-armed fatalities across the wider 16-day episode.
uncertainty_note: Government-side deaths are weakly sourced and likely undercounted; this is the main reason the episode is only tier C.
quality_note: Useful but notably less secure than the civilian numerator.

formula_note: Direct civilian deaths taken directly from the OHCHR/UNAMA episode press release.
uncertainty_note: The figure is preliminary and province-wide; denominator matching therefore uses city-plus-periphery scenarios.
quality_note: Strong count, but geography is broader than the ideal city-only episode footprint.

formula_note: No separate unknown-status death series found.
uncertainty_note: Unknown-status deaths were strictly excluded rather than allocated.
quality_note: Null by design.

formula_note: Average armed-presence scenarios of 3,500 / 5,500 / 7,500 over 16 days, reflecting Malkasian's smaller actual-fighter account and Reuters' larger nominal defender count.
uncertainty_note: This is a reconciled scenario range, not a direct roster count, because nominal city garrison and effective fighting strength differ sharply in the sources.
quality_note: Adequate for scenario analysis but not a precision denominator.

formula_note: Average civilian-presence scenarios of 180,000 / 250,000 / 320,000 over 16 days, using city population as center and displacement/peri-urban spillover to widen bounds.
uncertainty_note: The numerator is province-wide while the denominator is city-plus-periphery; this mismatch is the main driver of denominator uncertainty.
quality_note: Transparent but approximate matching denominator.

formula_note: Low=(mil_low/mil_pm_high)/(civ_high/civ_pm_low); best=(mil_best/mil_pm_best)/(civ_best/civ_pm_best); high=(mil_high/mil_pm_low)/(civ_low/civ_pm_high).
uncertainty_note: The point estimate is informative, but the interval is wide because organized-armed deaths are incomplete and the civilian denominator must approximate a province-wide numerator with a city-centered footprint.
quality_note: Treat as a scenario ratio, not a stable exact statistic.

formula_note: Auxiliary subgroup metric from report snippet; only the deaths portion is retained here.
uncertainty_note: The snippet says 'children' and the u18 mapping is explicit but inferential because the PDF could not be directly checked.
quality_note: Keep as auxiliary, not as a replacement for the full civilian series.

formula_note: Auxiliary subgroup metric from report snippet; only the deaths portion is retained here.
uncertainty_note: Snippet-based rather than directly PDF-verified in this environment.
quality_note: Auxiliary subgroup series only.

formula_note: Direct civilian casualties as published by OHCHR/UNAMA.
uncertainty_note: Casualties include both deaths and injuries and are not interchangeable with deaths.
quality_note: Useful auxiliary severity metric.

formula_note: Direct civilian injuries as published by OHCHR/UNAMA.
uncertainty_note: Reported for Kunduz province rather than city-only.
quality_note: Auxiliary injury series only.

formula_note: 13 U.S. service members + 1 ISIS-K suicide bomber = 14 organized-armed deaths.
uncertainty_note: This excludes any additional Taliban or ISIS-K gunmen because later official reviews concluded the attack was a single-bomber event and did not confirm other armed fatalities.
quality_note: Numerator is clean for known armed fatalities but limited in scope.

formula_note: Official minimum civilian fatality count from the Abbey Gate investigation.
uncertainty_note: Early same-week media reports were lower, but later official reviews converged around the 170-minimum figure.
quality_note: Strong numerator, weak denominator.

formula_note: No separate unknown-status death count isolated for the bombing itself.
uncertainty_note: Pre-bombing airport deaths from mixed causes are kept in context claims, not folded into the bombing numerator.
quality_note: Null by design.

formula_note: Average armed presence scenarios of 5,000 / 6,000 / 6,400 over 1 day, converted using 30-day months.
uncertainty_note: Airport-wide troops are only a proxy for those directly exposed to the Abbey Gate corridor, so the denominator is conservative and episode-specific precision is low.
quality_note: Modeled from airport-wide troop reporting rather than gate-specific rosters.

formula_note: Average exposed civilians at Abbey Gate modeled as 800 / 1,500 / 3,000 for one day, informed by throughput and crowd reports but not directly observed.
uncertainty_note: This is the weakest estimate in the bundle: throughput and crowding evidence do not reveal a precise point-in-time gate headcount.
quality_note: Heavily model-generated by necessity.

formula_note: Low=(mil_low/mil_pm_high)/(civ_high/civ_pm_low); best=(mil_best/mil_pm_best)/(civ_best/civ_pm_best); high=(mil_high/mil_pm_low)/(civ_low/civ_pm_high).
uncertainty_note: All scenarios are well below 1 because civilian death density in the gate corridor was far higher than military death density on the bombing day; exact magnitude is highly denominator-sensitive.
quality_note: Interpret only as a bounded micro-theater scenario ratio.
